SOFTWARE TEAMS Calendars are team APIs They expose when people can talk, when focus should be protected, and when stakeholders get predictable access. When can we talk? When should we not interrupt? When do stakeholders get access? 5 slides for teams that need both flow and focus 01 / 05 solutions; not code 01 Protect build time Architecture, debugging, reviews, and delivery need uninterrupted blocks. If deep work is not scheduled, it gets consumed. Reserve focus blocks before the week fills up Keep them visible, not overly detailed Treat them as real commitments 02 / 05 solutions; not code 02 Make collaboration predictable The goal is not no meetings. The goal is fewer surprise interruptions and clearer windows for conversation. Cluster team syncs into agreed windows Use office hours for ad-hoc technical questions Prefer async when no decision is needed 03 / 05 solutions; not code 03 Give stakeholders clear access Stakeholders should not have to chase the team. They also should not fragment every developer's day. Offer recurring stakeholder windows Share realistic review and decision availability Route urgent topics through one escalation path 04 / 05 solutions; not code 04 Make boundaries explicit A good calendar creates trust: available means available, focus means focus, and emergencies stay rare. Use clear labels: Focus, Review, Pairing, Stakeholder Sync Mark focus time as busy Make collaboration possible without sacrificing focus 05 / 05 solutions; not code
